name: M.ImputedValue_Mean
label: Mean Imputation
description: Computes the arithmetic mean of all non-missing observed values within a group as the imputed value. Common for baseline covariates or when a group-level central tendency is an appropriate replacement.

formula.notes: The partition defines the scope of the mean computation. For subject-level mean imputation, partition by subject. For group-level, partition by treatment group. The mean excludes the missing values being imputed.

methodInput.name: value
methodInput.description: Source values from which the mean is computed

methodInput.name: group_partition
methodInput.description: Grouping variables defining the mean computation scope (e.g., treatment group, site, or subject)

assumptions: At least one non-missing value exists in the group
Missing data are Missing Completely at Random (MCAR) or Missing at Random (MAR) for unbiased mean estimation
Mean imputation underestimates variance — not suitable when variance estimation is important
